Details: One bench-scale study [2745] evaluated the effectiveness of four types of naturally occurring adsorptive clay minerals for treatment of anatoxin-a at neutral pHs and ambient temperatures. The absorptive clays were moderately effective, with removal varying as a function of the clays adsorbent capacity and surface area (from 34 to 92 percent removal). The most effective clay mineral appeared to be kerolite which had the highest surface area and adsorptive capacity.
